Author: Micheal Lanham
Title: Can Vibe Coding Work for Production Code?
Text:
Member-only story
Can Vibe Coding Work for Production Code?
An Honest Look at Using AI to Write Your Software
Vibe coding — building software by instructing an AI through simple human language — is the hot new trend making waves across the developer community. But can we really trust AI-generated code in a production environment? Let’s dive into the good, the bad, and the practical to find out.
What Exactly is Vibe Coding?
Vibe coding is essentially programming by giving instructions to AI language models like GPT-4, Claude, or GitHub Copilot, instead of writing every line yourself. You describe what you want, and the AI provides the actual code snippets.
Andrej Karpathy, an AI pioneer, humorously described it as, “It’s not really coding — I just see stuff, say stuff, run stuff, and copy-paste stuff, and it mostly works.” This casual, conversational approach makes vibe coding attractive to developers who want to prototype rapidly. It blends traditional programming with natural language interaction, making it accessible and intuitive for those who might feel intimidated by traditional coding practices.
